name: Seila Law LLC v. Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
question:
1. Does the vesting of substantial executive authority in the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, an independent agency led by a single director, violate the separation of powers principle?
  2. If it does, is 12 U.S.C. § 5491(c)(3) severable from the Dodd-Frank Act?
description: A case in which the Court held that the vesting of substantial executive authority in the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, an independent agency led by a single director, violates the separation of powers, but the statutory provision creating that violation, 12 U.S.C. § 5491(c)(3) is severable from the Dodd-Frank Act.
